pipeline_description: Footprint reclamation hybrid approach: each confirmed junction's 95th-percentile spatial radius is computed; 'No Junction' records falling within that radius are reassigned to the nearest junction before HDBSCAN clustering of the remainder. 11,788 mislabeled points reclaimed into confirmed junctions.
dataset_description: Bengaluru Traffic Police (BTP) illegal parking violations, Jan–May 2024

peak_enforcement_hours.narrative: On average, 86.93% of violations per hotspot occur during defined peak hours (08:00–10:00 and 17:00–20:00), reflecting commuter-period congestion patterns.

headline_insights: The top-ranked hotspot is 'BTP051 - Safina Plaza Junction' with an impact score of 81.18 and 17,001 violations.
168 of 4208 hotspots are confirmed BTP junctions; 4040 were newly discovered via clustering.
35,585 vehicles have been cited more than once; the most chronic offender has been cited 55 times.
Street-grouped HDBSCAN with compound street+locality key prevents road-network chaining — a spatial clustering failure where a continuous road fuses into one artificially large false cluster.

Impact scores combine violation frequency (30%), congestion-severity weighted violation type (30%), peak-hour concentration (20%), and average vehicle footprint weight (20%), with a confidence discount for low-volume hotspots.
Footprint reclamation reassigned 11,788 'No Junction' records into their nearest confirmed junction before clustering.

methodology_notes.street_grouping_key: Compound street+locality key (first and last clean address segment) to prevent cross-neighborhood merging
methodology_notes.junction_first_strategy: 168 BTP named junctions used as ground truth; 'No Junction' records clustered separately
